## Architectural Rules

*Derived from: `check-boundaries.mjs`, `check-route-boundaries.mjs`, `package-registry.mjs`*

### `RULE-LAYER-ORDER`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs (main loop) + LAYER_TIERS

No package may import from a package at a higher layer index than its own.

> Enforced in CI via `node scripts/check-boundaries.mjs`. Violations exit(1).

### `RULE-SAME-LEVEL-PEERS`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs + ALLOWED_SAME_LEVEL_PAIRS

Peer imports at the same layer index are forbidden unless the pair appears in ALLOWED_SAME_LEVEL_PAIRS.

> Allowed pairs: @brandos/governance-layer→@brandos/iskill-runtime | @brandos/iskill-runtime→@brandos/governance-layer

### `RULE-1 — apps/web cognition-client isolation`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs checkWebBiWriteRule() (v6)

`apps/web` must NOT import `@brandos/cognition-client` directly. All cognition access routes through CPL proxy functions.

> CPL proxies: getBrandMemory() [throws — no CognitionProvider equivalent], recordBrandMemoryObservation(), reviewBrandMemorySignal(), resolveBrandCognitionContext(), getBrandSummary(), ingestWorkspaceKnowledgeAsset(). Exception: apps/web/instrumentation.ts (process bootstrap) constructs and registers the singleton once, at startup, via dynamic import().

### `RULE-2 — OCL cognition-client isolation`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs checkOclBiRule() (v6)

`@brandos/output-control-layer` must NOT import from `@brandos/cognition-client`.

> OCL receives all cognition data via ResolvedGenerationContract / ContributorContext.cognitionContext, populated by CPLOrchestrator. No direct cognition-client import permitted.

### `RULE-3 — CPL cognition-client symbol allowlist`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs checkCplBiRule() + CPL_COGNITION_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS (v6)

`@brandos/control-plane-layer` may only import specific symbols from `@brandos/cognition-client`.

> Allowed: getGlobalCognitionClient, initCognitionClient, setGlobalCognitionClient, createDegradedCognitionContext, getGlobalKnowledgeIngestClient, initKnowledgeIngestClient, plus the CognitionContext/CognitionProvider type family re-exported from @platform/cognition-contract. HttpCognitionProvider and DegradedCognitionProvider (concrete classes) are deliberately excluded — see RULE-6/RULE-7.

### `RULE-4 — ARL ↛ OCL (Fix C1)`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs checkArlOclRule()

`@brandos/ai-runtime-layer` must NOT import from `@brandos/output-control-layer`.

> ARL is a domain-agnostic runtime kernel. Artifact prompts delivered via registerArtifactPrompt() through globalThis.__brandos_runtime_adapter bridge.

### `RULE-5 — GL ↛ OCL (Fix C2)`
**Source:** check-boundaries.mjs checkGlOclRule()

`@brandos/governance-layer` must NOT import from `@brandos/output-control-layer`.

> repairJSON / extractJSON moved to @brandos/shared-utils. Import from there.

### `RULE-6 — retired, folded into RULE-3 (v6)`
**Source:** formerly check-boundaries.mjs checkCplBiRepositoryRule() (Fix C4)

(Retired) Formerly: `@brandos/control-plane-layer` must NOT import concrete BI repository classes. Under the current HTTP-adapter architecture, IntelligenceOS's repository layer is not visible to BrandOS at all — this concern has no analog. See RULE-3.

> No longer independently enforced; RULE-3's allowlist already excludes anything this rule would have caught.

### `RULE-7 — retired, folded into RULE-3 (v6)`
**Source:** formerly check-boundaries.mjs checkCplBiConcreteClassRule() (Fix C3)

(Retired) Formerly: `@brandos/control-plane-layer` must NOT import BrandIntelligenceRuntime concrete class as a value. Current equivalent: CPL must not import HttpCognitionProvider/DegradedCognitionProvider as values — use createDegradedCognitionContext() / getGlobalCognitionClient() instead. Enforced as part of RULE-3's allowlist, not a separate check.

> No longer independently enforced; RULE-3's allowlist already excludes anything this rule would have caught.

### `RULE-OCL-GOVERNANCE-CONFIG`
**Source:** monorepo Rule 7 + WS2 + OCL dependencyBoundary.test.ts

`@brandos/output-control-layer` must NOT import from `@brandos/governance-config`.

> Structural constraints now sourced directly from @brandos/contracts (Cleanup Sprint 2 WS2). Enforced by tests/boundary/dependencyBoundary.test.ts inside OCL.

### `RULE-PL-AUTH-ISOLATION`
**Source:** monorepo Rule 9 + WS1 + PL dependencyBoundary.test.ts

`@brandos/presentation-layer` must NOT re-export `@brandos/auth` symbols from src/index.ts.

> Auth state injected via PLAuthProvider/PLAuthContext. Consumers import directly from @brandos/auth.

### `RULE-ROUTE-BOUNDARY`
**Source:** check-route-boundaries.mjs + FORBIDDEN_IN_ROUTES

Next.js route files in apps/web/app/api/ must NOT import runtime layers directly.

> Forbidden in routes: @brandos/governance-layer, @brandos/ai-runtime-layer, @brandos/output-control-layer, @brandos/artifact-engine-layer, @brandos/iskill-runtime, @brandos/cognition-client

### `RULE-ADMIN-AUTH`
**Source:** monorepo Rule 12

Every /api/admin/* route must call requireAdmin() before any logic.

### `RULE-NODEJS-RUNTIME`
**Source:** monorepo Rule 13

Next.js route files that touch CPL must export `const runtime = "nodejs"`.

### `RULE-ARTIFACT-ENGINE-NO-TOUCH`
**Source:** monorepo Rule 11

`engine.ts` and `registry.ts` inside @brandos/artifact-engine-layer are read-only for agents.

> These are hard no-touch zones. Modifications require explicit human approval.
